title: 2010_laboratory_investigations_of_low_swirl_injector_operating_with_singas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Okay, here's the technical content extracted from the provided images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   S = (2/3)*tan(alpha) * (1 - R^3) / sqrt(1 - R^2 + [m^2(1/R^2 - 1)^2]R^2)
*   1 - (dU(x - xo)/dx) / (Uo) = (ST/Uo) + (SL/Uo) + (Ku'/Uo)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1 (LSI prototype for Taurus 70 engine)**
    *   Components- labels: Center Channel, Swirl Annulus
    * Dimensions: 3.05 mm, 3.26 mm, 3.45 mm
*   **Figure 2 (Schematics of the LSI setup for atmospheric studies)**
    *   Components- labels: Venturi Premixer, LSI swirler, Fuel (in flow direction arrow), Air (in flow direction arrow), Flow Settling Chamber, PIV seeds

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Swirl Number (S): 0.4 - 0.55, 0.51
*   'alpha' (vane angle): 40 deg
*   R (center-channel to injector radius ratio): 0.63
*   m (ratio between mass fluxes): 0.39
*   LSI internal radius: 3.175 cm
*   LSI Diameter: 6.35 cm
*   Center-channel Diameter: 4 cm
*   Swirler recess: 9.5 cm
*   Settling chamber diameter: 25.4 cm
*   Straight feed-tube length: 30 cm
*   Laminar flame speeds (SL): 0.2-0.3 m/s
*   Maximum fuel supply capability: 65 g/s
*   Bulk flow velocity (Uo): 9.6 m/s, 22 m/s
*   PIV Laser pulses: 120 mJ
*   PIV Laser wavelength: 532 nm
*   PIV camera resolution: 2048 x 2048 pixels
*   PIV field of view: 13 x 13 cm^2
*   PIV resolution: 0.065 mm/pixel

Okay, here's the technical data extracted from the provided text and image:

**1. Mathematical equations:**

*   R = 0.55
*   alpha = 45 (deg)

**2. Graphs/charts:**

**Figure 3(a): Lean Blow-Off Limits (delta_LBO  vs U0)**

*   Axes:
    *   x-axis: U0, U m/s
    *   y-axis: delta_LBO
*   Curves plotted:
    *   0.  5CH4/0.5CO2
    *   CH4
    *   SG1 - 0.2H2/0.4CO/0.4CH4.
    *   SG2 - 0.3H2/0.3CO/0.4CO2
    *   SG3 - 0.6H2/0.4CO
    *   H2

**Figure 3(b): Lean Blow-Off Limits (Tad  vs U0)**

*   Axes:
    *   x-axis: U0, U m/s
    *   y-axis: Tad, K
*   Curves plotted:
    *   0.  5CH4/0.5CO2
    *   CH4
    *   SG1 - 0.2H2/0.4CO/0.4CH4.
    *   SG2 - 0.3H2/0.3CO/0.4CO2
    *   SG3 - 0.6H2/0.4CO
    *  H2

**3. Tables:**

**Table 1: Syngas compositions and conditions for the PIV experiments**

| Name | Composition          | phi  | Tad (K) | S (m/s) |
|------|-----------------------|------|---------|---------|
| SG1  | 0.2 H2-0.4 CO-0.4 CH4 | 0.52 | 1591    | 0.14   |
|      |                       | 0.58 | 1708    | 0.18   |
| SG2  | 0.3 H2-0.3 CO=0.4 CO2  | 0.55 | 1575    | 0.14   |
|      |                       | 0.64 | 1698    | 0.22   |
| SG3  | 0.6 H2-0.4 CO         | 0.45 | 1586    | 0.22   |
|      |                       | 0.5  | 1696    | 0.29   |

**4. Diagrams:**

No diagrams are visible in the image.

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

*   Al2O3 particle size: 0.6-0.8 µm
*   Frequency: 10 kHz
*   Light sheet thickness: 1.1 mm
*   Waist (spherical lens): 0.3 mm
*   Interframe time: 25 µs
*   Image pairs: 224
*   PIV interrogation region: 64x64 pixels
*   Overlap: 25%
*   Spatial resolution: approximately 2 mm
*   Quartz cylinder length: 30 cm
*   Quartz cylinder diameter: 20 cm
*   NO in nitrogen calibration gas: 7.9 ppm

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the extracted technical content from the provided research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Au = S\_L * (tau - 1)
*   a\_t = delta U/ delta x / U\_0
* q' = ((u'^2 + v'^2)^(1/2)) / 2

**2. Graphs:**

*   **(a) NOx, ppm (@15% O2) vs. Tad (K)**

    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: NOx, ppm (@15% O2) - Logarithmic scale, ranging from 10^0 to 10^2
        *   x-axis: Tad (K) - Linear scale, ranging from 1200 to 2200
    *   Curves represent different fuel compositions:

        *   NG T = 671K, P, = 12 atm
        *   NG T = 720K, P, = 15 atm
        *   CH4, STP
        *   C2H6, STP
        *   C3H8, STP
        *   0.6 CH4/0.4 H2 , STP
        *   0.5 CH4/CO, STP
        *   0.2 H2/0.4 CO/0.4 CH4, STP
        *   0.3 H2/0.3 CO/0.4 CO2, STP
        *   0.6 H2/0.4 CO STP
        *   0.5 H2/0.5 CO STP
*   **(b) CO ppm (@15% O2) vs. Tad (K)**

    *   Axes:
        *   y-axis: CO ppm (@15% O2) - Linear scale, ranging from 0 to 140
        *   x-axis: Tad (K) - Linear scale, ranging from 1200 to 2200
    *   Curves represent different fuel compositions :

        *   NG T = 671K, P, = 12 atm
        *   NG T = 720K, P, = 15 atm
        *   CH4, STP
        *   C2H6, STP
        *   C3H8, STP
        *   0.6 CH4/0.4 H2 , STP
        *   0.5 CH4/CO, STP
        *   0.2 H2/0.4 CO/0.4 CH4, STP
        *   0.3 H2/0.3 CO/0.4 CO2, STP
        *   0.6 H2/0.4 CO STP
        *   0.5 H2/0.5 CO STP
* **Figure 5 : Normalize velocity vectors**

    * UV / u'v'
    * X/D
    * Y/D

**3. Tables:**

*   The text mentions a table 1 listing mixtures used for PIV experiments. Table is not visible on this cropped page.

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 5: Normalized Velocity Vectors**

    *   Three sub-diagrams:  SG1-0.2 H2/ 0.4 CO/0.4 CH4, SG2-0.3 H₂/0.3 CO/0.4 CO₂, SG3-0.6 H₂/0.4 CO

title: 2010_laboratory_investigations_of_low_swirl_injector_operating_with_singas
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the gas turbine research page:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   S<sub>T</sub>/S<sub>L</sub> = 1 + 3.15 u'/S<sub>L</sub>
*   S<sub>T</sub>/S<sub>L</sub> = 1 + 1.73 u'/S<sub>L</sub>

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 8: Turbulent Flame Speed Correlation**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: u'/S<sub>L</sub> (unitless)
        *   y-axis: S<sub>T</sub>/S<sub>L</sub> (unitless)
    *   Curves:
        *   Data points for H2, 0.5 H2 / 0.5 CO, 0.75 H2 / 0.25 N2, CH4, 0.6 CH4 / 0.4 N2, 0.5 CH4 / 0.5 CO2, 0.6 CH4 / 0.4 H2, 0.75 H2 / 0.25 CH4, 0.5 CH4 / 0.5 H2, SG1, SG2, SG3
        *   Correlation for H2, Correlation for CH4
    *   Key Values: No key values or thresholds are explicitly stated.

*   **Fig. 10: Equivalence Ratios of the High Pressured Syngas Experiments**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: % H2 (percentage)
        *   y-axis: phi, equivalence ratio (unitless)
    *   Curves: shows the distribution of data points.
    *   Key Values: Equivalence ratios varied from 0.3 to 0.55

*   **Fig. 9: Syngas Compositions Evaluated at a 75 cm ID Pressurized Combustor**
    *   Axes and Triangle corners:
        *   CO: Corner of triangle
        *   H2: Corner of triangle
        *   CH4: Corner of triangle
    *   Curves: shows the distribution of data points.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   Nozzle exit velocity: 20-40 m/s
*   Temperature (T): 450 K < T < 505 K
*   Pressure (P): 1.5 atm < P < 14 atm, P=8 atm
*   Thermocouple mounted
*   Camcorder: 30 fps
*   Hydrogen concentration (H2): 20% < H2 < 56%, 15%, 51%
*   Carbon Monoxide concentration (CO): 25% < CO < 60%
*   Methane concentration (CH4): 19% < CH4 < 51%
*   Equivalence ratio (phi): 0.28, 0.3-0.55
*   Adiabatic flame temperature (Tad): 1350 K < Tad < 1800 K
*   Optimum operating range restricted to U<sub>0</sub> < 20 m/s

        *   **(b)** 20% H2, 40% CO, 40% CH4, phi = 0.28, T = 509K and P = 7.8 atm, U0 = 31.8 m/s
